success_surface: Row filtering based on cell value criteria while preserving workbook structure and formatting
effective_behaviors: Data exploration before processing to understand actual value types (strings vs. error objects)
Bottom-to-top iteration when deleting rows to prevent index shifting issues
Explicit preservation of header/reference rows during row manipulation
Post-processing verification to confirm expected rows were retained/removed
String-based pattern matching for Excel error representations (e.g., '#N/A') rather than relying on non-existent object attributes
Iterative debugging with fallback logic when initial implementation attempts fail
evidence: Agent printed unique values in target column to discover '#N/A' exists as string, not error object
Final solution iterates from max_rows down to 2, avoiding header row modification
Verified retained rows match expected criteria after execution
Multiple failed attempts revealed .error attribute doesn't exist on openpyxl Cell objects
Used str().strip().lower() comparison instead of direct equality for robustness

memory_items.title: Pre-Processing Data Exploration
memory_items.lesson: Inspect actual cell value types and content before implementing filtering logic
memory_items.generalization: Always examine sample values and their Python types to understand if special Excel values (like errors, dates, formulas) are stored as native types or strings.

memory_items.title: Safe Row Deletion Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When deleting multiple rows, iterate from highest to lowest index to prevent incorrect shifts
memory_items.generalization: Use descending row iteration (max_row to min_row) for row deletions; skip reference/header rows explicitly rather than assuming row 0 or 1.

memory_items.title: Excel Error Detection Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Some Excel errors appear as strings ('#N/A', '#DIV/0!') rather than native error objects in openpyxl
memory_items.generalization: Check both string representations and error object types when identifying special values; use flexible string matching with normalization (case-insensitive, stripped whitespace).

memory_items.title: Verification Before Completion
memory_items.lesson: Confirm expected outcomes by inspecting retained/modified data after execution
memory_items.generalization: Always print or log summary statistics after bulk operations: row counts, retained criteria values, affected ranges.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Row Filtering Operations
- **Explore first**: Inspect target column values and types before implementing filter logic
- **Delete safely**: Iterate rows from bottom to top (max to min) to prevent index shifting
- **Preserve headers**: Skip row 1 (or specified reference rows) explicitly in your range definition
- **Error handling**: Excel error symbols (like #N/A, #DIV/0!) may be stored as strings; check both string matches and error object types
- **Verify outcomes**: Print retention summaries and row counts after bulk modifications

### Debugging Common Pitfalls
- openpyxl Cell objects do NOT have an `.error` attribute - use value checks instead
- Cell `.style` objects may not have predictable `.name` attributes - test carefully
- When VBA-style instructions reference "errors", check if they're actually stored as text strings
